Your Healing Kitchen
Natural healing · Fourth trimester
Herbal tea • Postpartum recovery • Digestion & milk

Caraway Tea

A warm seed tea that eases bloating and gently supports your milk.
Makes 1 cup
Time 10 min
Caffeine none
For you · after meals
When to drink After meals — especially if you feel bloated or gassy.

Why this heals you

  • Caraway — a traditional digestive; calms bloating & wind tradition
  • A gentle milk-support herb, like fennel tradition
  • Warming & soothing on a tender gut tradition
  • Caffeine-free tradition
Ingredients
1 tspCaraway seeds (lightly crushed)
1 cupJust-boiled water
optionalA little honey (to taste)
Method
1
Crush

Lightly crush 1 tsp caraway seeds to release the oils.

2
Steep

Pour over a cup of just-boiled water; cover and steep 5–10 min.

3
Sip

Strain and sip warm — a little honey if you like.

Just for you, Mum

A cup after dinner settles a heavy, bloated tummy — and gently supports your supply while you nurse.

Good to know
BestAfter meals, warm.
ReboilYou can steep the seeds a second time.
Why we say this
Caraway as a digestive and milk-supporting herb is long-standing tradition (carminative seed family); labelled tradition, not a medical claim.
Main Meals
Snacks
Drinks
Desserts
Food, not medical advice · non-diagnostic · made for this season of motherhood
